audit_result.proof_trace: ComputeForkDigest only uses fork_version and genesis_validators_root (lines 304-310), ignoring blob parameters entirely. For Fulu (EIP-7892), fork digests must incorporate blob params via XOR with hash(epoch||max_blobs). The network schedule correctly implements this (config.go:576-590), but ComputeForkDigest bypasses it. When bootnode or other code paths call ComputeForkDigest directly for Fulu, networks with same fork_version but different blob params produce identical digests, breaking network isolation.
audit_result.attack_scenario: Attacker configures bootnode for Fulu fork with different MaxBlobsPerBlock than the legitimate network but same ForkVersion. ComputeForkDigest (bootnode.go:221) produces identical fork digest. Victim nodes connect to attacker's network thinking it's the same chain, leading to consensus failures, incompatible block processing, and potential chain split.

PROP-56ad1eb2-inv-027.text: Fork digest must differ when blob parameters change, even if fork version is unchanged (EIP-7892).
